lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Had any luck finding someone to slay the demon?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Oh! Yes! The demon, with the oppression and stuff!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: No, not yet. Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because I came to offer to help.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: That's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: When I am reinstated in my rightful place, I will not be a very wealthy man, as my half-brother has squandered my family fortune.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: However, I will gladly give you every bent penny of what is left, and starve in the gutter with my many adorable children if you say you will help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No reason. I was just in the area and I thought I would ask.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Look, I think I'll wait here for someone a little more... you know...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: ...heroic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Mornin'.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Hey...you're an adventurer, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I am!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got any quests for me?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Yeah, I do, as a matter of fact!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: (Ahem.)

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: I am a poor, dispossessed nobleman, forced by circumstance to lurk in the middle of nowhere, soliciting help from passers-by.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: You see, my fiendish half-brother has seized my estates and forced me into exile.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: The simple lemon farmers suffer under his tyrannous yoke, and only a brave adventurer can lift his iron boot from the neck of the poor.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: To reclaim my lands I will need to have my family sword returned to me so that I may present it as proof of my rulership.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Will you help me find my family sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Your uncorroborated sob story has touched my heart. When do we set off?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: You'll help! Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: But, alas, my half brother has a powerful ally, the mighty demon...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Err...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Err...Barrelor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Barrelor?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: That's what I said!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Barrelor is an awesome opponent, and to reclaim my family sword you will need to defeat him, for he guards it within the deadly Trapped Pit of Barrelor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, I fear no demon!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Atta [boy/girl]!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: When I am reinstated in my rightful place, I will not be a very wealthy man, as my half-brother has squandered my family fortune.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: However, I will gladly give you every bent penny of what is left, and starve in the gutter with my many, many adorable children, if you say you will help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nonsense! Keep the money! I will dispose of this evil half-brother of yours and leave you what little money is left to feed your family.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Wonderful! Just pick up your diversion and we'll leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What diversion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'll take the cash in used coins please.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Look, I think I'll wait here for someone a little more... you know...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: ...heroic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a chance, this sounds too dangerous.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Look, I think I'll wait here for someone a little more... you know...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: ...heroic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't think I'll help you out this time.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Look, I think I'll wait here for someone a little more... you know...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: ...heroic.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arrr... 'Tis lookin' bleak...

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Good news Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: I found us a hero down by the docks!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Be they heroic, brave and true?

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Aye! They also be gullible, tied up and unconscious!

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: They were willing to help out some random stranger with a good enough sob story, so I smacked them with a bottle and rowed them over.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Brilliant! The island's location will remain a secret!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Bring 'em here and wake 'em up.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: We may make it through this yet...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Are ye alright, [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ohhhh... My head...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It feels like someone has smacked me one with a bottle...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr... Those devils gave ye a nasty knock when ye came to aid us.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: But now yer here we'll run those evil brain-eatin' dogs off the island fer good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? What is going on here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't seem to remember anything beyond chatting to a man at the docks.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr. Well, [lad/lass], that would be Pete, one of my men.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: He's been out lookin' fer heroes like yerself to aid us in our peril.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I'll buy that. It sounds like something I would do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where am I, and what is going on?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Yer on Braindeath Island!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Where it lies is a secret, because ye are standin' in the brewery of Cap'n Braindeath, purveyor of the most vitriolic alcoholic beverages in the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I am the notorious alchemist Cap'n Braindeath, and this whole operation be my idea!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: With my crew of sturdy, upright pirate brewers, we sail the seven seas, distributing cheap 'alcohol' to all and sundry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooh!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: These be dark times, though, [lad/lass]. See, a week ago we awoke to find ourselves beseiged.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: The lads and I have held them off so far, but 'tis only a matter of time before they sweep through the buildin' and put us all to the sword.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are they...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...protesting?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, [lad/lass]! That they are!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Day and night they seek to break our will with their chantin', and their singin' and their passive resistance!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, me and the lads got our heads together and decided that if we can get their Cap'n drunk enough, perhaps they'll stop protestin'.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: If that happens, we'll slip out the back and set up shop somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, first of all, we need someone to go out the front and grow us some Blindweed.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: 'Tis one of the ingredients of our 'rum'.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: The only problem is that those rottin' fiends have torn up and destroyed all but one of the Blindweed Patches.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here, [lad/lass]. I'll give ye the seed you'll need fer growin' the herb. Help yerself to the gardenin' equipment in the basement.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I'll warn ye again that those devils are sat right on top of the patch.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Try hecklin' 'em from a distance. Those Swabs may talk a good fight, but if ye can put a scare in 'em they'll keep out of yer way!

sections.title: Using the blindweed seed on Captain Braindeath

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do with this?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, well, I want ye to get outside and grow some Blindweed.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Best be careful, lad, fer them pirates will skin ye alive if they catch ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, well I want ye to get outside and grow some Blindweed.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Best be careful, [lad/lass], fer them pirates will skin ye alive if they catch ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what happens if I lose the seed?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Then I'll have ye flogged, hung, slapped with a haddock and sent back out there to fight the zombie hordes with little more than harsh language!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Come back and see me when ye ain't loaded down with stuff, and I'll give ye another.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr... Here, [lad/lass]. Just take this and get goin'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you talk like a pirate? Didn't you tell me you were an alchemist?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, [lad/lass], 'tis true.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: However, 'tis also true that I stumbled across the basic recipe fer my most potent of brews in a terrible alchemical accident.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: See, 'twas a dark and stormy night, and the wind was howlin' around the trees as I worked late into the night.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Steppin' too close to a candle with my flask in my hand, I was suddenly swept up in a terrible, yet potently alcoholic, explosion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well' [lad/lass], it seems the fumes from that first batch of me 'rum' did strange things to me brain.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I don't remember the exact words the healers used, but apparently the stuff burned out the tiny, specialised part of me brain that tells me not to talk like a pirate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That... that... that sounds utterly impossible!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr! That be what I told them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you make here anyway?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: 'Rum', [lad/lass]!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: The finest, most potent, most flammable and most debilitatin' 'rum' in the whole of Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rum, eh?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No, [lad/lass], 'rum'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the difference?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, see, it's like this.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: If we called the stuff we make 'rum' without makin' the little quote gestures every time, then the Cookin' Guild has promised to do entertaininly painful things to us with whisks.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: See, technically -

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: And by that I mean technically according to the Disposal of Hazardous Waste Act and the Health and Safety Laws -

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: technically, what we're brewin' here is Artificially Produced Hyper Condensed Sweetened 'Rum' Flavour Distillate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiiiiiight...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: So ye see, [lad/lass], we just call it 'rum' because the real name be a bit of a mouthful.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Want a drop?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks... I think I'll skip it for now.

sections.title: Talking to Brewers around the facility

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Oh Guthix, who am I kidding?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: We're all gonna die!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: You're one of THEM, aren't you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: O...kay...walking away now, smiling and not making eye contact.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Game over, man! Game over!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr...keep up the good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: How do I know you're not one of them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't know... the way I'm breathing could give it away.

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: That means nothing! NOTHING!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Don't talk to me! If I play this right, I can sell you lot out and make it to the mainland!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: No school today mother, my brain hurts real baaaad...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: I no sleep in many, many, many days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Don't sneak up on me like that!

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Don't you know there's a war on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didnt think this counted as a war.

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: That's because you're a landlubber!

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: I bet ye've never had to beat a dozen zombies to death with a blunt toothpick!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. Have you?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: No, I've spent most of my time in the toilet to be honest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... how are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: Very well, considering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Considering what?

lines.speaker: Brewer
lines.text: That I've been crawling into the corner to cry whenever nobody is looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow... I always thought pirates were tough...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: 'Ello guv.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you not helping out the others?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Well you see, guv, I've got these unshakable religious convictions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And those are?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Well, it's like this, see. I used to do a bit of the old priestin' on the side.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: You know, collectin' for repairs to the church roof, passing the collection plate, jumble sales, nicking the lead off the temple roof so they needed repairing again.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Real holy stuff, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound all that holy to me.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Well, that's because you're not a man of the cloth are you?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: You are, in fact, what we refer to in layman's terms as a punter.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: But anyway, all that priestin' left me with an unshakable faith in three things.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: The power of good over evil.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: The true glory of the human spirit.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: And that I ain't goin out there with those things runnin' around.

sections.title: Searching the basement cupboard for farming supplies

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, but...

lines.speaker: Zombie protester
lines.text: Arr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any way you could...

lines.speaker: Zombie protester
lines.text: ARRR!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anyone else I could...

lines.speaker: Zombie protester
lines.text: Arrrrrrrrrr!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fiiine...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arr! What be ye wantin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, err, came to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to tell you to leave.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Ye have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Hahahahahahahahahahahahaha!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Bwahahahahahahahahahahahaha!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Wheeze...wheeze...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Gadzooks [lad/lass], that be the funniest thing I've heard all day! Say it again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to tell you to leave.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Stop [lad/lass]! I'll shatter me ribs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to ask you what you want.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Whadda we want? 'Rum'! When do we want it? Now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...if I were to give you 'rum' you would leave?

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Not really [lad/lass]. If ye were to give us 'rum' we'd kill ye quickly, as opposed to over a few weeks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to join your crew!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr! Shiver me mainbraces and make them landlubbers walk the scurvy plank, Cap'n! I've come to join yer cut- throat, bilge swillin' crew! Also, arr!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Are ye quite done, [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, for the time being anyway.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Well, ye'll be glad to know that after that little performance I'd be glad to have ye on me crew!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huzzah!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Course, I'll have te kill ye first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Don't ye worry, [lad/lass]. After we take the island I'll have the boss haul yer body to the temple and...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Err, never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind what?

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Ferget I said anything.

sections.title: Trying to talk to Zombie Swabs
Trying to sneak past 50% Luke too early

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Arr! Tryin' ter get away eh? Well ye'll never sneak past me, I'm the best lookout this crew has ever seen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...look...an astonishing...thing. There. Behind you.

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Ye'll have te do better than that, landlubber!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Hey yerself, landlubber!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...so that you will know exactly what time it is due to the horrible pain in your earlobes!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Arr! Prepare to die!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...meaning you'll need a dozen Ogres and a team of highly skilled surgeons to remove them!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Hahahaha!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Yer goin ter die!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...so that all you will be able to hear from then on will be the rustling of the ants walking through them!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Yer brave, but stupid!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and then I'll use a wrench to remove the handles, so you'll see it every time you cough!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Who? Me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and I'll grind up what's left, mix it with lemon juice and throw the whole lot into the ocean!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: Yer a deadman, sez I!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...leading to the eventual, violent explosion of those organs!

lines.speaker: Zombie swab
lines.text: That be the most intimidatin' thing I've ever heard! I'm not messin' with you!

sections.title: If Zombie Swab roams away during your insults
Trying to re-intimidate a Zombie Swab
Attempting to rake a trashed patch
After the Blindweed has fully grown
Talking to Captain Braindeath after getting blindweed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here! I have your Blindweed!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Splendid, [lad/lass]! Go shove it in the Intake Hopper upstairs.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: We'll beat them zombies yet! Arr!

sections.title: If the player has lost the blindweed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain... I grew some blindweed but then I lost it...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: There there, lad. 'Tis all right.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here, I've been savin' some fer an emergency.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Go shove that in the Intake Hopper, and try not ter lose this lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go put yer Blindweed in the intake hopper, and to be sharp about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what happens if I lose the Blindweed between here and the intake hopper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Theoretically, of course, as it would be almost impossible for me to simply lose the stuff in the time it will take me.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Gah!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Yer nothin' better than a sea-slug wearin', lilly-legged, bandy-livered landlubber!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I've got a little put aside fer an emergency. Drop some of that junk yer carryin', and come see me to get it.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here, I was savin' a little fer an emergency, so take this and shove it in the hopper before I bung ye in there and turn the handle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You had some all the time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why was I risking my neck killing pirates to grow some if you had some all along?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Less talk, lad, haven't ye got a hopper to be fillin'?

sections.title: Before putting blindweed in the hopper

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go put yer Blindweed in the intake hopper, and to be sharp about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what happens if I lose the Blindweed between here and the intake hopper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Theoretically, of course, as it would be almost impossible for me to simply lose the stuff in the time it will take me.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Gah!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Yer nothin' better than a sea-slug wearin', lilly-legged, bandy-livered landlubber!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I've got a little put aside fer an emergency. Drop some of that junk yer carryin', and come see me to get it.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here, I was savin' a little fer an emergency, so take this and shove it in the hopper before I bung ye in there and turn the handle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You had some all the time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why was I risking my neck killing pirates to grow some if you had some all along?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Less talk, [lad/lass], haven't ye got a hopper to be fillin'?

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath after using blindweed on the hopper

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that takes care of the Blindweed. What now?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, now ye've shoved the Blindweed into the mix, what we need is a bucket of stagnant water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get some of that?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye won't have to go far, [lad/lass], we have a pool of the stuff here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here in the brewery?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No, [lad/lass], that would be a strange thing to have in a brewery!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: It's up the mountain to the north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Up a mountain?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, 'tis technically a volcano, but ye get the general idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I assume the place is crawling with these zombies?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No [lad/lass], there be not a zombie in sight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, good!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: All ye have to do is get past the keen-eyed lookout that's been spottin' my men when I send 'em.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I'll tell ye that it won't be easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When is it ever...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Don't ferget yer bucket!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath again before getting stagnant water

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: To get off yer lazy behind and head out to the stagnant lake on top of the volcano.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Chop chop, lad.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Don't ferget yer bucket!

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Arr! Tryin' ter get away eh? Well ye'll never sneak past me, I'm the best lookout this crew has ever seen!

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Where?

sections.title: Using a bucket on Stagnant Lake

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Hey! What are you doing out there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: Well Cap'n Donnie said no livin' landlubbers were allowed out of the compound.

lines.speaker: 50% Luke
lines.text: So get yerself back in here, or yer for it!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath before pouring in the stagnant in the hopper

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go pour yer bucket into yonder hopper.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Take yer time, lad, we're only bein' besieged by zombies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Captain...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Aye, what is it, lad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, on my way over to the hopper some big boy stole the bucket and ran away...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, 'tis a good job I saved a little, isn't it? Now get rid of something so I can give it to ye.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Give me strength!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: If ye weren't so good at distractin' the zombies I'd skin ye alive!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Take this bucket and pour it into the hopper. If ye don't get it right this time I'll have yer guts fer garters!

sections.title: Using bucket of water in the hopper
Talking to Captain Braindeath after using the water on the hopper

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is that everything?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No, [lad/lass], next ye'll need to go outside and catch five loads of Sluglings fer the brew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Sluglings? That's disgusting!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, [lad/lass], that it be.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: To a weak-stomached, knock-kneed landlubber...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why Sluglings?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: 'Cos, [lad/lass] they're one of our super-secret ingredients! Yer not too susceptible to mind control are ye [lad/lass]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Because they have been known te, well, influence people every now and again.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I'm sure ye've got nothing te worry about!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They aren't related to those Seaslugs are they?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No [lad/lass], we just call them 'Sluglings' 'cos of a long and convoluted story involvin' a metal pipe and three dead seagulls.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Aye they're related! Tis a good job they'll starve if they tried te eat yer brains!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I do with these Sluglings anyway?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, Ye shove them in the Pressure Barrel in the attic.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: And then ye...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then I...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Pressurise 'em.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pressurise them?!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Just a little.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Look, we don't have all day, get movin'.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye'll be needin' a fishbowl in a big net to help ye catch them slimy little blighters.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Pity ye ain't got any free space to carry one.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here. Ye'll need this to catch them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do with it?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Just dunk it in the water. I'm sure a clever [lad/lass] like yerself will have no problem.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Oh, and if ye haul up some squiddy-looking things, don't hesitate to shove 'em in the barrel too.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: They add a special, fishy texture to the drink.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath again before catching the sluglings

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go outside and catch five sea creatures from the squid fishin' spot.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: When ye've grabbed them, jam 'em in the barrel in the attic and pressurise 'em.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I suppost to catch these things?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye'll be needin' to use a fishbowl in a big net to snare these little devils.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if I don't have one?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Why, I oughta...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Come see me when yer not so laden down. I've got another one fer ye if ye need it.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Here! Try not ter lose this one! These things don't grow on trees, ye know.

sections.title: Using a fishing spot without fishbowl and net
Using a fishing spot without inventory space
Using a fishing spot without level 50 Fishing
Using a fishing spot with the correct requirements

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Gah! Get that slimy thing away from me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The slugs are talking to me... They tell me to do bad things...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Gimme that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Egad... what just happened?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: What happened is ye were messin' about with Sluglings!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Don't ye have anything better to do?

sections.title: Attempting to stuff a sixth Slugling into the barrel
Trying to pull the lever with less than five sea creatures
Pulling the lever with 5 sea creatures in the barrel
Talking to Captain Braindeath after filling the pressure barrel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How could I kill my sluggy brethren...?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Snap out of it, [lad/lass]! Yer in slug-shock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gah! Sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: No problem. Well, now ye have just one more ingredient to grab, and then we can get this 'rum' flowin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, how far away will I have to go to grab it?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Not far at all, [lad/lass]. Ye've just got to get it from the basement!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What is it?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Hold yer horses, [lad/lass]! While ye was off gallivantin' with yer slimy aquatic playmates, the 'rum' achieved spiritual critical mass.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: To put it in terms ye'll understand: the brewin' equipment is possessed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possessed!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Don't ye worry yerself about it! This happens all the time.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, to tell the truth, my lads are a little quicker off the mark, so it only happens occasionally.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Not that I'm criticisin' yer performance, [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Give the controls a couple of belts with this wrench.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: One of the lads did a little priestin' on the side before he came here. Get him to bless it and ye'll do fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want yeto go clear the Evil Spirit out of the brewin' controls. Use the wrench I gave ye, but get it blessed first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mumblemumblewhatwrenchmumble...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: What were that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I said, what if I've lost the wrench?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, ye landlubber!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Calm down, remember yer ulcer...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Look, I've got another wrench, but ye don't have any free space fer it. Come back when ye do.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Arr! I'd get more work out of a minty flavoured brick!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: If ye lose this one I'll have ye turned inside out, covered with spiders and turned rightside in so they'll eat ye alive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Steady on!

sections.title: Using a non-holy wrench on Brewing control

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come out and face me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You used to be a priest, right?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: I didn't nick anything, guv. I've got twenty people who'll swear blind I was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind. I need some help with the spirit in the brewing equipment.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Well, I'll bless something for you if you need it, but just don't spread it around that I'm giving out freebies.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Trust me, guv, lots of people need help after coming into contact with the spirits we produce here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you bless this wrench for me?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Nope. You don't strike me as being... devout, you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come again?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Your holy aura, it's a little shoddy, if you catch my drift.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: You need a Prayer level of 47.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well why didn't you just say so?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: I might have had a bit of an overestimation problem guv. I'm sure it won't happen again.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: I might well do. Remember, only the first one's free.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Dominoes Ad Nauseum, Romanes Eunt Domus.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Sorted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Oh, you want the full package deal.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: All right. Brace yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Groovy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: No problem, guv. Good luck with your little problem.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: You might find that little wrench worth hanging on to after you're done with the Spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? I mean, it's holy and everything, but I don't think it looks all that useful.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: Well, it may not look much, but you'll find that you might need a few less prayer potions if you have it in your pack, if you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, no. I don't know what you mean, but I'm sure I'll find out!

sections.title: Talking to Davey after getting the holy wrench

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You used to be a priest, right?

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: I didn't nick anything, guv. I've got twenty people who'll swear blind I was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind. I need some help with the spirit in the brewing equipment.

lines.speaker: Davey
lines.text: You mean more help? I've already blessed one wrench for you, that should be enough.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath with the holy wrench

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go clear the Evil Spirit out of the brewin' controls. Use the wrench I gave ye, but get it blessed first.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I see ye've got the wrench good and blessed, so go whack the controls till the Evil Spirit pops out.

sections.title: Using the holy wrench on Captain Braindeath

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I see ye've got the wrench good and blessed, so go whack the hopper 'til the Evil Spirit pops out.

sections.title: Using the holy wrench on the brewing control
Using the holy wrench on the controls when the spirit is already out

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The power of Guthix compels you!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath after killing the evil spirit

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, now that ye've got that spirit out of there ye can dump in the final ingredient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that is?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: We need the body of a diseased Fever Spider!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me never to drink anything you have ever made. Or touched.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: When yer quite done flappin' yer lips, go down into the basement and whack spiders until ye find a fever spider body.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Shove it in the hopper, and then we're in business.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye may want to hop back to the mainland and see a Slayer Master.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: These Fever Spiders carry a nasty disease, and ye'll need a pair of Slayer Gloves on to not catch it!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I see yer already wearin' some Slayer Gloves. That'll keep the Fever Spiders from gnawin' yer hands off!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath again before getting the spider corpse

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: I want ye to go kill a Fever Spider and jam its body in the hopper.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Whenever yer ready, [lad/lass].

sections.title: Using the spider corpse on Captain Braindeath

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: What are ye doin', [lad/lass]? I want ye to shove that in the hopper, not in me face!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye just can't get good help these days...

sections.title: Using the spider corpse on the hopper
Talking to Captain Braindeath after using the spider carcass on the hopper

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I stuck your spider in the hopper, what now?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Now ye stand well back and watch the glory of brewin' at its best!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Aye [lad/lass]! Now get outside and feed that stuff to the pirates.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Try givin' it to the Captain, he's in charge. Get him bladdered and the rest will fall!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Ye'll need to use that bucket of yours. Most stuff can't stand bein' in contact with our 'rum' fer too long.

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Took us a lot of dissolvin' to work that one out.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath before talking to Captain Donnie

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: So, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: The Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh! I haven't spoken to him yet!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Well, get a move on!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: And don't ferget the 'rum'!

sections.title: Turning the output tap (requires a bucket)

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Be that the finest, most abrasive 'rum' I've ever smelled?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! That it be!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Hand it over or I'll run ye through!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arr.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Ye be a good [lad/lass], fer a filthy livin' landlubber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I take it your boss will be pleased?

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arr, that he will. I'll tell...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Wait a minute...

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arr, ye tricky dog!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Ye tried to trick old Donnie!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, I guess I'll have to try again.

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arr, [lad/lass], you tried to trick me, but i was too clever for ye!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Besides, Rabid Jack would have my hide if I told ye it were him that sent me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure he would. Good job you caught me out, eh!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Aye! Now get ye gone, and don't return without more 'rum'!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Donnie again while having 'rum' with you
Trying to get more 'rum' from the Output tap

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Hey you! I can smell the 'rum' on ye!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: If it isn't in my belly by the time I cout ter three, I'll have ye flogged!

lines.speaker: Captain Donnie
lines.text: Arrr...that hits the spot...

sections.title: Talking to Captain Braindeath after talking to Captain Donnie

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: So, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much that was coherent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Rabid Jack?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Rabid Jack!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: THE Rabid Jack!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Egad...I haven't heard that name...

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: ...before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, who is he?

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Dunno [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Almost as if I aren't changing subjects, well done!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: With those rottin' dogs legless they'll never keep fighting us now, so we've decided to stay here and keep the 'rum' flowin!

lines.speaker: Captain Braindeath
lines.text: Thanks, [lad/lass]. We'd never have managed without ye!
